title: Assessment scorecard report
description: Data contract for the scorecard format: a PERIODIC ASSESSMENT SCORECARD. Assessment areas are graded cards/rows, each scored 0-100 against a small set of criteria with RAG (ok/risk/hold) ratings, a trend arrow, and a weight that drives its contribution to a single overall grade under a banner. This is areas x criteria, graded for a period (NOT a live monitoring console, NOT an options x criteria decision matrix). Renders to a centred (~1500px), themeable, interactive HTML scorecard. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown above the title, e.g. 'Quarterly delivery assessment' or {{REPORT_TYPE}}.

title.description: Main scorecard title, e.g. 'GEN2 programme health scorecard'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst describing what is being assessed and the assessment basis.

date.description: ISO date the assessment was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. The in-report 9-dot switcher (Black mandatory) can change it live.

scale.description: The 0-100 scoring scale and the grade bands every score (area scores and the overall) is bucketed into. Bands define the RAG-style tiers (e.g. A/B/C/D or Strong/Stable/Watch/At risk) and colour the score chips, gauges and overall banner.

min.description: Lowest possible score on the scale (typically 0).

max.description: Highest possible score on the scale (typically 100).

bands.description: Grade bands from worst to best (or best to worst — render order follows the array). A score falls in the first band whose [min, max] range it satisfies; bands should tile the scale without gaps.

label.description: Band label / grade letter shown on chips and the banner, e.g. 'A', 'Strong', 'At risk'.

min.description: Inclusive lower bound of this band on the scale. A score qualifies for the highest band whose min it meets or exceeds.

max.description: Optional inclusive upper bound. If omitted, the band extends to scale.max (top band) or up to the next band's min.

tone.description: Semantic tone driving the band colour: 'ok' (cleared/strong), 'risk' (bordeaux/at risk), 'hold' (amber/watch), or 'brand' (neutral brand accent).

areas.description: The assessment areas — one graded card (and one row) each. Each area carries a 0-100 score, a weight (relative importance), a period trend arrow, and the criteria it was graded on. The overall grade is recomputed from these (weighted or raw, per the live toggle).

id.description: Stable unique key for the area (used for sort/filter/expand state). e.g. 'delivery'.

name.description: Area display name shown on the card header and row, e.g. 'Delivery & scope'.

summary.description: Optional one-line assessor note summarising this area's standing for the period.

icon.description: Optional house icon key for the area header (falls back to a generic mark): 'delivery', 'quality', 'risk', 'finance', 'people', 'security', 'process', 'data', 'generic'.

weight.description: Relative importance of this area (any positive scale, e.g. 1-10 or a percentage). Drives the weighted contribution to the overall score; the template normalises weights so they need not sum to 100.

score.description: This area's assessed score on the scale (scale.min..scale.max). Bucketed into a band for its chip and gauge.

prevScore.description: Optional score for this area in the previous period; powers the per-area delta (Δ) shown when 'period compare' is on.

trend.description: Period-over-period direction shown as an arrow: 'up' (improving ▲), 'down' (declining ▼), 'flat' (steady ▬).

criteria.description: The graded criteria for this area, revealed when the area is expanded. Each is a RAG-rated line item with its own optional sub-score and note.

rating.description: RAG rating for this criterion: 'ok' (green/met), 'risk' (bordeaux/breached), 'hold' (amber/at watch), 'na' (not assessed / not applicable). Drives the criterion dot and the RAG filter.

score.description: Optional sub-score on the scale for this criterion (shown as a mini bar). Purely indicative; the area score is authoritative.

note.description: Optional short evidence / qualifier shown on hover and in the expanded panel.

overall.description: The headline assessment banner: the overall grade and score for the period, plus the optional previous-period score that powers the headline delta. The score may be authoritative (assessor-set) or treated as a check against the data-recomputed total shown by the template.

grade.description: Overall grade label shown large in the banner, e.g. 'B+', 'Stable'. Should correspond to a scale band.

score.description: Overall assessed score on the scale for this period. The template also computes a weighted/raw roll-up from areas and flags any divergence.

period.description: The assessment period this scorecard covers, e.g. 'Q2 2026' or 'May 2026'.

prevScore.description: Optional overall score for the previous period; drives the banner Δ (▲▼ vs last period) and enables the 'period compare' control.

prevPeriod.description: Optional label for the previous period, e.g. 'Q1 2026', shown alongside the delta.

statement.description: Optional 1-2 sentence overall assessor statement shown under the banner grade.

meta.title: Programme health scorecard
meta.subtitle: Quarterly assessment of the GEN2 delivery programme against weighted health areas.
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

areas.name: Delivery & scope
areas.summary: Velocity recovered after the integration freeze; two epics slipped to next quarter.

areas.name: Quality & defects
areas.summary: Escaped-defect rate rose during the ITb monkey-test phase; regression suite stabilising.

criteria.label: Escaped defect rate
criteria.note: P1 escapes above target this period

areas.name: Risk & compliance
areas.summary: Regulatory gap assessment closed; one residual audit action carried over.

areas.name: Budget & forecast
areas.summary: On budget at the half-year; vendor change-request pipeline a watch item.

areas.name: Team & capacity
areas.summary: Key roles filled; reliance on two specialists remains a single-point risk.

overall.statement: The programme is stable and improving overall, lifted by risk and team areas, but quality has regressed during the ITb test phase and is the priority focus for Q3.
